reasoning: Multiple reports since January 2025 have consistently indicated that South Korea and Vietnam are in the 'final stages' of securing an agreement for the export of 20 K9 self-propelled howitzers. A late March 2025 report reiterates that Vietnam is 'set to purchase' the howitzers for around $276-$300 million. While some sources claim the deal was already signed, the most reliable phrasing indicates that the final contract is undergoing standard government-to-government bureaucracy and is pending an official signature or announcement. Given that the first units are expected to be delivered within 2025 (according to March 7 reports), the official signing is highly likely to take place in the near term. With two months left before the July 1, 2025 deadline, the probability of an official announcement is strong, though there remains a minor risk of bureaucratic delays pushing the formal signature into H2 2025.
